title: tragacantha californica
intro: Tragacantha californica, also known as the California buckthorn or California soap bush, is a species of shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used to make herbal preparations, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and treating skin conditions. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also sometimes used in topical applications.
